body:
Title: CRA Form T2222 2025 Northern Residents Deductions.

Source:
- https://www.canada.ca/content/dam/cra-arc/formspubs/pbg/t2222/t2222-25e.txt

CRA T2222 2025 calculates northern residents deductions for line 25500. Normalized numeric values used by the encoded formulas include 11.00, 5.50, 0.20, 1200, 0.50, 23, and 69. The instructions state that generally, individuals who lived on a permanent basis in a prescribed northern zone or prescribed intermediate zone for a continuous period of at least six consecutive months may claim the deduction; Zone A residents may claim the full amount and Zone B residents may claim one-half of the deductions.

Step 2 calculates the residency deduction. Line 1 multiplies the number of days in a prescribed northern zone by $11.00. Line 2 multiplies qualifying additional residency days in a prescribed northern zone by $11.00. Line 3 adds line 1 and line 2. Line 4 is non-taxable benefits for board and lodging at a special work site in a prescribed northern zone. Line 5 subtracts line 4 from line 3 and floors the result at zero. Line 6 multiplies days in a prescribed intermediate zone by $5.50. Line 7 multiplies qualifying additional residency days in a prescribed intermediate zone by $5.50. Line 8 adds line 6 and line 7. Line 9 is non-taxable benefits for board and lodging at a special work site in a prescribed intermediate zone. Line 10 subtracts line 9 from line 8 and floors the result at zero. Line 11 adds line 5 and line 10. Line 12 multiplies line 23600 net income by 20%, encoded as 0.20. Line 13 is the lesser of line 11 and line 12.

Step 3 calculates the travel deduction. Chart A row C says the total allocated standard amount for each individual cannot be more than $1,200. Chart B has two other-travel trip slots and a medical-travel slot. For each Chart B trip, column 3 is either the taxable travel benefit or the portion of the individual's $1,200 standard amount allocated to the trip, column 4 is travel expenses, and column 5 is the lowest return airfare. The form enters the lowest amount from columns 3, 4, or 5 in Column 6 for Zone A or Column 7 for Zone B. Amount A adds all Column 6 Zone A amounts. Amount B adds all Column 7 Zone B amounts. Line 14 is Amount A. Line 15 is Amount B multiplied by 50%, encoded as 0.50. Line 16 adds line 14 and line 15. The instructions set the simplified meal rate at $23 per meal, to a maximum of $69 per day per person, when the simplified method is used.

Step 4 calculates northern residents deductions. Line 17 is the residency deduction from line 13. Line 18 is the travel deduction from line 16. Line 19 adds line 17 and line 18 and is entered on line 25500 of the return.
